rationale: The citing paper uses Chandon and Wansink (2002) in its conceptual development of the consumption-acceleration mechanism, specifically to support the claim that salient, stockpiled products are consumed faster. This is part of the paper's theory-based argument for why club-store bulk purchasing can raise total packaged-food purchases across formats. The citation therefore plays a meaningful framing role beyond a generic background mention, even though the 2018 study does not directly reuse the 2002 methods.
relation_description: The 2018 paper draws on Chandon and Wansink (2002) as conceptual support that stockpiling and salience can speed consumption. That prior insight is used as a foundation for the citing paper's mechanism linking club-store shopping to higher overall packaged-food quantities.
